basic_details.job_title: Manipur Horticulture Department Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 282 Peon, Chowkidar and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Department of Horticulture & Soil Conservation, Government of Manipur, has thrown open 282 contract-basis vacancies spread across nine different posts - from Assistant Agriculture Officer to Peon and Lab Attendant. Issued on 10 June 2026 under File No. DH&SC-1/835/2026(Pt.1), the notification invites applications from eligible, Manipur-domiciled candidates sponsored through the local Employment Exchange. Online forms open on 15 June 2026, submissions begin 16 June 2026, and the last date to apply is 30 June 2026. Candidates can Apply Online at the department's official portal, and the orig

important_dates.exam_date: To be intimated in the Admit Card

application_fee.details: Fee amounts differ by post category and reservation status. PwD candidates are exempt from fees across every post.

qualification.details: This section answers the most basic question first: can you even apply? The department has laid down two non-negotiable conditions that apply to every single post in this drive, regardless of qualification or category. | Candidates must be a citizen of India and a permanent resident of the State of Manipur. Candidates must be able to speak Manipuri or any of the notified Scheduled Tribe dialects of Manipur. Applicants must be sponsored by the concerned Employment Exchange to be considered eligible. The engagement is purely contractual - running from the date of the contract agreement up to the close of the financial year, and extendable from time to time at the department's discretion. | Beyond these common conditions, each post carries its own educational qualification and age bracket, detailed in the table below.

vacancy_details.Minimum Qualification: B.Sc (Agriculture/Horticulture) from a recognized University or equivalent
PU Science or equivalent from a recognized University
Graduate with a Course on Computer Concepts (CCC) - DOS, Windows, MS Office, Multimedia, Internet - from a Central/State recognized Institute
Class X / HSLC or equivalent from a recognized Board/Institution

how_to_apply: Here's the exact application process as laid down by the department, from downloading the form to submitting it with fees.
Visit the official Directorate website: www.horticulture.mn.gov.in.
Download the prescribed application form for your chosen post - note that no hard copy is issued from the office, so the online form is your only option.
Fill in the form carefully and attach all relevant supporting documents, duly attested or self-attested.
Pay the prescribed application fee applicable to your post and category (see fee table above) - PwD candidates do not need to pay any fee.
If applying for more than one post, repeat the process and pay fees separately for each application, while applying to only one post per Group (B, C, or D).
Submit the completed form online within the application window - 15 June to 30 June 2026.
Retain your submission acknowledgment; the Admit Card will be issued at a later date with exam details.

You can Apply Online directly through the Directorate's official website once the form link goes live, and the complete notification is available as a PDF Download Link on the same site for reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Manipur Horticulture Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for issue and submission of the application form is 30 June 2026. Online forms become available from 15 June 2026, with submissions starting 16 June 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in this recruitment?
faq.answer: There are 282 vacancies in total, spread across 9 posts: Assistant Agriculture Officer/Equivalent, Field Assistant/Equivalent, LDC/Office Assistant-cum-Computer Operator, Fieldman, Mali, Peon, Peon-cum-Chowkidar, Chowkidar, and Lab Attendant.

faq.question: Q3. Who is eligible to apply for these posts?
faq.answer: Applicants must be Indian citizens and permanent residents of Manipur who can speak Manipuri or a notified Scheduled Tribe dialect of Manipur, and must be sponsored by the concerned Employment Exchange.

faq.question: Q4. What is the minimum qualification for Assistant Agriculture Officer?
faq.answer: A B.Sc in Agriculture or Horticulture from a recognized university or its equivalent, with an age limit of 21 to 38 years as on the date of notification.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee, and is it waived for any category?
faq.answer: Yes, fees range from ₹300/- to ₹1,000/- depending on the post and category. PwD candidates are exempt from the application fee for every post in this notification.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for these posts?
faq.answer: Selection is written test plus viva voce for Assistant Agriculture Officer (Group B), and written test only for all other posts under Group C (Field Assistant, LDC/OACCO) and Group D (Fieldman, Mali, Peon, Peon-cum-Chowkidar, Chowkidar, Lab Attendant).

faq.question: Q7. Can a candidate apply for more than one post?
faq.answer: Yes, but only one post per Group (Group B, C, or D), and a separate application form with separate fees must be submitted for each post applied to.

faq.question: Q8. What is the nature of this job - permanent or contractual?
faq.answer: This engagement is purely on contract basis, valid from the date of the contract agreement up to the close of the financial year, and extendable from time to time.
